title: Business Analytics
summary: Data-driven insights for strategic and operational decision-making.
description: A practice-oriented course that develops the ability to frame business questions, analyse data, interpret models and communicate evidence for managerial action.
audience: Business management, engineering management and early-career professionals who need practical analytical decision skills.
prerequisites: Basic spreadsheet skills; no advanced programming required.
outcomes: Frame business problems as answerable analytical questions.
Prepare and examine structured business data.
Select suitable descriptive, predictive and prescriptive approaches.
Interpret analytical results for managerial decisions.
Communicate recommendations through clear visual and written narratives.

modules.title: Business questions and analytical thinking
modules.description: Problem framing, decision context and measures of success.

modules.title: Data preparation and exploration
modules.description: Data quality, summaries, patterns and visual analysis.

modules.title: Predictive analysis for management
modules.description: Forecasting, classification and practical model interpretation.

modules.title: Communicating analytical recommendations
modules.description: Dashboards, executive narratives and implementation choices.

rightsNotes: Third-party trademarks, images, datasets and quoted material remain subject to their respective rights.
